description: Accessibility Enhancements (items 81-90): Screen reader announcements, high contrast mode, font size adjustment, dyslexia-friendly font toggle, reduced transparency mode, focus mode, voice navigation, captions for audio previews, motion sensitivity levels, and color blind modes.
steps: 1. Enhance screen reader announcements: add aria-live regions that announce 'Now playing: Song Name by Artist' when track changes. Add to MiniPlayer.tsx (already has one - verify it works). Add announcements for queue changes and favorites.

2. Add high contrast mode to settings: create a toggle in app/components/settings/AppearanceSettings.tsx that adds 'high-contrast' class to html element. Define high-contrast CSS overrides in globals.css with maximum contrast ratios (pure black/white).

3. Add font size adjustment to settings: create a slider in AppearanceSettings that adjusts base font size (14px-22px range). Apply via CSS variable --user-font-size on document root. Use rem units throughout.

4. Add dyslexia-friendly font toggle: add OpenDyslexic font via @font-face in globals.css. Toggle in settings adds 'dyslexia-font' class to html, which sets font-family to OpenDyslexic. Load font from Google Fonts or CDN.
5. Add reduced transparency mode: toggle in settings that adds 'reduce-transparency' class to html. CSS rules disable all glass/blur effects, replace with solid opaque backgrounds.

6. Create app/components/FocusMode.tsx: a toggle that hides everything except current content (no sidebar, no bottom nav, no player, no FAB). Only shows main content area. Toggle via keyboard shortcut and settings.

7. Create app/components/VoiceNavigation.tsx: use Web Speech API for voice commands - 'play next', 'pause', 'search for [query]', 'go to favorites'. Parse speech recognition results and trigger appropriate actions.

8. Enhance audio previews with captions: when audio plays, show track title and artist as text overlay/banner (like closed captions). Create app/components/player/AudioCaption.tsx visible when audio is active.

9. Enhance motion sensitivity in settings: expand the existing reducedMotion/animationSpeed settings to offer Off / Reduced / Normal / Full levels. 'Off' disables all animations, 'Reduced' uses prefer-reduced-motion styles, 'Full' enables all effects.

10. Create app/components/ColorBlindMode.tsx: add color blind mode options (Protanopia, Deuteranopia, Tritanopia) in settings. Apply CSS filter (hue-rotate + saturate adjustments) or custom color palette overrides to the root element.
acceptance_criteria: Screen readers announce track changes via aria-live regions
High contrast mode provides pure black/white maximum contrast
Font size is adjustable from 14px to 22px via settings slider
Dyslexia-friendly font (OpenDyslexic) is available as toggle
Reduced transparency removes all glass/blur effects
Focus mode hides all chrome leaving only main content
Voice navigation processes commands like 'play next' and 'search for'
Audio captions display track info when preview plays
Color blind modes adjust colors for Protanopia/Deuteranopia/Tritanopia
verification: bun run lint passes with zero errors
bun run build succeeds with zero errors
findings: All 10 accessibility features implemented and verified. React 19 strict linting required avoiding setState in effects and ref mutations during render - used module-level state and DOM manipulation via requestAnimationFrame for screen reader announcements. OpenDyslexic font loaded from jsdelivr CDN. Color blind SVG filters use feColorMatrix. Focus mode hides elements via CSS class on html element. Voice navigation uses Web Speech API with graceful fallback. Build and lint both pass with zero errors.
